text.title: figma-vs-adobe-strategy-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for figma-vs-adobe-strategy-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Applying a product-team comparison to a creative agency workflow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Best tool" conclusions do not transfer across team types. A product team prioritizes collaboration and dev handoff; a creative agency prioritizes asset creation and multimedia output. Use team-type-specific weights.

failure-mode.description: Including pricing data from training data in a recommendation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pricing structures change. Always fetch current pricing from official sources before including it in a recommendation — per-seat vs. bundle calculations depend on team size and current plan tiers.

failure-mode.description: Referencing AI feature names rather than capabilities in automation scripts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both platforms have changed their AI feature names multiple times since 2024. Prompt patterns should reference capabilities (e.g., "text-to-vector generation") not product names (e.g., "Firefly Draw") that may be renamed.

failure-mode.description: Assuming Adobe Creative Cloud API is headless for automation pipelines.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adobe CC automation via UXP requires a human session inside a CC app. Purely headless pipelines must use Firefly Services (enterprise only). Standard CC plans have no headless automation surface.
